title: S & SH Sounds
focus: Crisp, clean S and SH - don't let them blur into each other.
twisters: She sells seashells by the seashore.
Six sleek swans swam swiftly southwards.
The sixth sick sheikh's sixth sheep's sick.
Shy Shelly says she should share her sushi.
Sheila's sushi shop sells fresh salmon sandwiches.
Silly Sam saw seven silver ships sailing swiftly.
The seashore was scattered with small, smooth stones.
Sally's silk scarf slipped silently to the sidewalk.
Sam's sister Sue sews six soft cushions.
Shane should share his snacks with his sisters.
The shepherd's sheep scattered across the sunny slopes.
Sarah's spicy soup surprised the whole seaside restaurant.

title: R & L Sounds
focus: A common mix-up - keep R and L clearly separate, especially at speed.
twisters: Red lorry, yellow lorry, red lorry, yellow lorry.
Really leery, rarely Larry.
The lazy lion roared loudly near the river.
Round the rugged rock the ragged rascal ran.
Larry's red car rarely runs late on rainy roads.
The little lizard slowly crawled along the rocky ledge.
Rory really likes rare, ripe raspberries and lemons.
Laura's letter arrived later than the rural mail truck.
The rolling river ran rapidly around the rugged rocks.
Really rural roads rarely have reliable lighting.
Leo loves listening to loud rock and roll records.
The royal lawyer read a lengthy letter aloud.

title: TH Sounds
focus: Put your tongue between your teeth for TH - don't swap it for T or D.
twisters: Think of three thin things.
That thing on the third shelf belongs to them.
The thirty-three thieves thought they thrilled the throne.
This thick thread is thoroughly thin.
Theo thought thoroughly through thirty math theorems.
The theater thrived through three thrilling theatrical thrillers.
Thelma thanked them for the thoughtful, thorough thank-you note.
Three thousand theories thrived throughout the thick textbook.
This theme thoroughly threatens the thriving theater's future.
Thatcher thought the thin thread would never thread through.
The thoughtful therapist thanked three thankful clients thoroughly.
Their thirty-third theory thrilled the thoughtful theologians.

title: P & B Sounds
focus: P is a sharp puff of air, B is a soft vibration - feel the difference.
twisters: Peter Piper picked a peck of pickled peppers.
Betty bought a bit of butter, but the butter was bitter.
A big bug bit a big black bear.
Please pack up the plastic pickup packet.
Bobby's purple backpack packed perfectly into the black bin.
Paula baked a batch of plump, plain biscuits.
The bright bumblebee buzzed past the blooming peonies.
Peter's puppy playfully bit the bright blue ball.
Barbara's baby brother babbled busily by the pond.
The plump pigeon perched proudly beside the busy bakery.
Ben's best buddy brought bagels for breakfast Tuesday.
Paul's purple pants perfectly matched his brand-new boots.

title: V & W Sounds
focus: One of the trickiest for English learners - V touches your teeth, W doesn't.
twisters: We wondered whether the weather would worsen.
Vivian's video went viral very quickly.
Wendy's wolf watched Vincent's van vanish.
Very well, we will visit the village on Wednesday.
Vera's van veered west on the winding valley road.
William's velvet vest was very well received at the event.
The weary vet visited the vast, wild wildlife reserve.
Winnie wondered why the vivid violet wildflowers wilted.
Victor's watch vibrated wildly during the vigorous workout.
We wove a vivid, vibrant web of wildflowers.
Wesley's van won the vintage vehicle show downtown.
Vivienne waved warmly at the very wise vicar.

title: CH & J Sounds
focus: CH is a sharp, unvoiced sound; J is soft and voiced - keep them distinct.
twisters: Chop shops stock chops.
Chesty chicken chewed cheese chunks cheerfully.
Jenny's giant jelly jiggled joyfully.
Which witch switched the Swiss wristwatches?
Charlie's cheerful children chased the chubby chipmunk.
Jack's jacket jammed in the giant, jagged gate.
The chef chopped cherries and chestnuts for the cheesecake.
Jenny juggled just enough jam jars for the picnic.
Chuck cheerfully checked each chapter of the chosen chronicle.
The joyful judge joked about the giant orange juice.
Charlotte's chatty chihuahua chewed through the cherry chair.
Jasper's giant jigsaw jumbled the jagged jungle pieces.

title: Fast K & G Sounds
focus: Sharp, quick consonants - great for building overall speaking speed.
twisters: A big black bug bit a big black bear.
Can you can a can as a canner can can a can?
Greek grapes, Greek grapes, Greek grapes.
Kindly keep the King's key in the kitchen.
Kate's cat caught a quick, clever gray kangaroo.
The gray goose gobbled the crisp golden corn kernels.
Kyle's kite got caught in the tall green garden gate.
Greg's grumpy goat grazed near the great gray gorge.
Karen quickly cooked a crispy, golden-crusted cake.
The gigantic gorilla grabbed a great, crunchy cookie.
Craig's kayak glided quickly across the calm cove.
Gina's golden garden gnome guarded the crooked gate.

title: Numbers & Multi-Syllable Words
focus: Long words trip people up - break them into syllables first, then speed up.
twisters: Thirty-three thieves thought they thrilled the throne throughout Thursday.
Eleven benevolent elephants elegantly ambled.
The statistical analysis required particularly meticulous methodology.
Unquestionably, the entrepreneurship opportunity was extraordinarily lucrative.
Approximately seventeen extraordinarily enthusiastic entrepreneurs attended.
The multidisciplinary university unnecessarily complicated the curriculum.
Consequently, the internationally renowned philanthropist contributed generously.
Fifty-four particularly punctual professionals participated proactively.
The extraordinarily hospitable receptionist articulately assisted everybody.
Undoubtedly, the meteorological predictions proved remarkably accurate.
Ninety-nine incomprehensible instructions unnecessarily confused everyone.
The philosophical undergraduate thoroughly misunderstood the terminology.

title: Tricky IT & Business Words
focus: The exact words that trip people up in real meetings - worth over-practicing.
twisters: Authentication, infrastructure, vulnerability, asynchronous.
The algorithm's architecture requires careful reconfiguration.
Specifically, particularly, comfortably, thoroughly.
The entrepreneur's collaborative infrastructure strategy was extraordinarily comprehensive.
Cybersecurity vulnerabilities require immediate, comprehensive remediation strategies.
The synchronous, asynchronous, and multithreaded architectures functioned simultaneously.
Stakeholders unanimously approved the interdepartmental collaboration methodology.
The scalable, distributed infrastructure architecture significantly improved reliability.
Quarterly reconciliation requires meticulous documentation and thorough verification.
The organization's proprietary authentication protocol required extensive reconfiguration.
Interoperability between microservices necessitates robust API documentation.
The procurement department's reconciliation methodology proved remarkably efficient.

title: Mixed Rapid-Fire Challenge
focus: Everything at once - the real test of clear, confident articulation.
twisters: How much wood would a woodchuck chuck if a woodchuck could chuck wood?
Fuzzy Wuzzy was a bear, Fuzzy Wuzzy had no hair.
I scream, you scream, we all scream for ice cream.
She sells seashells, Peter Piper picked pickled peppers, and the sixth sick sheikh's sheep is sick.
Betty Botter bought some butter, but she said the butter's bitter.
A proper copper coffee pot properly poured the perfect coffee.
Red leather, yellow leather, red leather, yellow leather, red leather.
Unique New York, unique New York, you know you need unique New York.
The big black bug bled black blood while the bold bald bear bit back.
Six thick thistle sticks, six thick thistles stick.
Rory's lorry rarely rolls, and Larry's lorry never rolls.
Truly rural, truly rural, truly rural, truly rural.

title: F & V Sounds
focus: F and V use the same mouth shape - F is a quiet puff of air, V adds a voiced buzz. Feel the vibration only on V.
twisters: Five fine friends found fresh vegetables.
Vera's five favorite films are very funny.
The ferry driver drove fervently through five villages.
Frank's van has four flat front tires.
Fiona's favorite flavor was fresh vanilla and fig.
Victor's five friendly ferrets frolicked in the vast field.
The frantic firefighter fearlessly faced the ferocious flames.
Vivian felt very fortunate to find such fine fabric.
Frank's favorite verse featured five vivid, vibrant flowers.
The vineyard's fertile fields flourished after the heavy fall rain.
Felix's van veered off the foggy, forested valley path.
Vera's favorite festival features fireworks, food, and folk music.

title: M & N Sounds
focus: Both are nasal sounds - M closes your lips together, N touches your tongue behind your top teeth.
twisters: Many men mentioned nine new numbers.
Nancy's mom made nine mini muffins.
The manager mentioned a minor new number nine.
My neighbor's name is Norman Newman.
Nine nervous nurses noted numerous minor medical numbers.
Molly's mother numbered nearly ninety miniature notebooks.
The morning meeting mentioned nine minor manufacturing numbers.
Nathan noticed many neighbors mingling near the market.
My mom made nutritious meals for nine noisy nephews.
Nina's namesake mentioned nine memorable moments naturally.
The nimble monkey nimbly maneuvered near nine mango trees.
Norman's new manager mentioned a noteworthy monthly memo.

title: Long & Short Vowel Sounds
focus: Vowel length changes meaning in English - stretch the long vowels and keep the short ones crisp and clipped.
twisters: The sheep sleep deep in the green field.
Six thick sticks stick in the ditch.
Blue moon, true tune, cool pool.
The cat sat flat on the black mat.
The bee flees the deep, green sea breeze.
Big pigs dig quick, thick ditches near the bridge.
The blue moose chews on smooth, cool fruit.
The fat cat sat on the flat black hat.
Green leaves feel free beneath the deep sea breeze.
The thin king brings six quick things to the ring.
The huge, blue tube flew through the smooth, cool room.
The bad lad had a nap on the black mat.

title: Consonant Clusters
focus: Blended sounds like STR, SPL and SCR need extra clarity - say them slowly first, then build up speed.
twisters: Strange strawberries stretch across the street.
Splash, splutter, and spring straight through the spray.
Three throngs threw three throws through three thrones.
Scream, scratch, and scramble through the scrubby brush.
Strong streams stream straight through the strange stream bed.
Splendid sprinklers splash and spray across the spring garden.
Scrappy scriptwriters scribbled scrambled scripts scrupulously.
Three strict strangers strolled straight through the strait.
Sprinting sprinters sprang swiftly through the springy sprawl.
The scrawny scrub-jay scratched the scruffy, scrubby scrubland.
Strict instructors strictly restructured the strenuous strategy.
Splashing sprinters sprinted through the splintered, spray-soaked street.

title: Question Intonation & Rhythm
focus: Yes/no questions rise in pitch at the end, statements fall - rhythm and melody matter as much as individual sounds.
twisters: Are you really ready to read already?
Would you rather wander or wonder, Wendy?
Should Sheila share her seashells, or shouldn't she?
Can Kim's kitten keep the King's kite, or can't it?
Won't Wendy wonder whether Walter went walking, or won't she?
Isn't it interesting that Iris insists on ironing instantly?
Didn't Debbie decide to divide the delicious dinner differently?
Wasn't William worried whether Wendy would wait, or wasn't he?
Shouldn't Sam share the shiny silver spoon, or shouldn't he?
Aren't Anna and Andrew always arguing about anything, or aren't they?
Couldn't Carla carefully carry the careless cat's carrier, or couldn't she?
Hasn't Harry always hated hearing horrible horror stories, or hasn't he?
